title: What to know about Peripheral Arterial Occlusive Disease before using medicinal herbs
intro: For individuals with Peripheral Arterial Occlusive Disease, specific herbal teas offer proven benefits. Ginkgo biloba extract, derived from the leaves of the ginkgo tree, enhances blood flow and alleviates symptoms by reducing blood viscosity. Horse chestnut seed extract, rich in anti-inflammatory agents, improves venous tone and lessens leg pain and swelling. Butcher's broom, containing ruscogenins, reduces inflammation, strengthens vein walls, and improves circulation. These scientifically validated herbal teas offer a natural, effective complement to traditional treatment approaches for Peripheral Arterial Occlusive Disease.
definition: Peripheral Arterial Occlusive Disease (PAOD) is a serious circulatory condition characterized by narrowing or blockage of arteries outside the heart and brain, most commonly in the legs. This occurs due to the buildup of fatty deposits, leading to reduced blood flow and oxygen supply to affected limbs. PAOD is crucial to understand because it elevates the risk of heart attack, stroke, and limb amputation. Early detection and management of PAOD are vital to prevent complications and maintain a high quality of life.
